---
title: "Traway Operations Analytics — Yash Tiwari"
page-layout: custom
---

```{=html}
<!-- NAVBAR — uses ../ paths because this page is in projects/ -->
<header class="navbar">
  <a href="../index.html" class="nav-logo">YT.</a>

  <nav class="nav-links">
    <a href="../index.html"    class="nav-link">Home</a>
    <a href="../projects.html" class="nav-link">Projects</a>
    <a href="../blog.html"     class="nav-link">Writing</a>
    <a href="../resume.html"   class="nav-link">Resume</a>
    <a href="../about.html"    class="nav-link">About</a>
  </nav>

  <div class="nav-right">
    <a href="https://github.com/rsm-ytiwari" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="nav-icon" aria-label="GitHub">
      <svg width="17" height="17" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
        <path d="M9 19c-5 1.5-5-2.5-7-3m14 6v-3.87a3.37 3.37 0 0 0-.94-2.61c3.14-.35 6.44-1.54 6.44-7A5.44 5.44 0 0 0 20 4.77 5.07 5.07 0 0 0 19.91 1S18.73.65 16 2.48a13.38 13.38 0 0 0-7 0C6.27.65 5.09 1 5.09 1A5.07 5.07 0 0 0 5 4.77a5.44 5.44 0 0 0-1.5 3.78c0 5.42 3.3 6.61 6.44 7A3.37 3.37 0 0 0 9 18.13V22"/>
      </svg>
    </a>
    <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/yash-tiwari-here/" target="_blank" rel="noopener" class="nav-icon" aria-label="LinkedIn">
      <svg width="17" height="17" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
        <path d="M16 8a6 6 0 0 1 6 6v7h-4v-7a2 2 0 0 0-2-2 2 2 0 0 0-2 2v7h-4v-7a6 6 0 0 1 6-6z"/>
        <rect x="2" y="9" width="4" height="12"/><circle cx="4" cy="4" r="2"/>
      </svg>
    </a>
    <button class="nav-icon" aria-label="Search">
      <svg width="17" height="17" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
        <circle cx="11" cy="11" r="8"/><line x1="21" y1="21" x2="16.65" y2="16.65"/>
      </svg>
    </button>
    <button class="theme-pill" id="theme-toggle" aria-label="Toggle theme">
      <span id="theme-icon">
        <svg width="13" height="13" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
          <path d="M21 12.79A9 9 0 1 1 11.21 3 7 7 0 0 0 21 12.79z"/>
        </svg>
      </span>
      <span id="theme-label">Dark</span>
    </button>
  </div>
</header>


<!-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     CASE STUDY BANNER
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ -->
<div class="case-banner">

  <a href="../projects.html" class="back-link">
    <svg width="14" height="14" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
      <line x1="19" y1="12" x2="5" y2="12"/><polyline points="12 19 5 12 12 5"/>
    </svg>
    All Projects
  </a>

  <div class="case-tag">Analytics · Entrepreneurship</div>

  <h1 class="case-title">Traway Operations Analytics</h1>

  <p class="case-subtitle">
    Cohort analysis, pricing segmentation, and retention modeling for a
    student-led mobility startup — turning 3,200 customers into actionable
    growth levers.
  </p>

  <div class="case-meta-row">
    <div class="case-meta-item">
      <span class="case-meta-key">Timeline</span>
      <span class="case-meta-val">Dec 2022 – Jul 2024</span>
    </div>
    <div class="case-meta-item">
      <span class="case-meta-key">Role</span>
      <span class="case-meta-val">Co-founder &amp; CEO</span>
    </div>
    <div class="case-meta-item">
      <span class="case-meta-key">Stack</span>
      <span class="case-meta-val">Python · SQL · Regression · EDA</span>
    </div>
    <div class="case-meta-item">
      <span class="case-meta-key">Outcome</span>
      <span class="case-meta-val">60%+ retention · 3× AOV lift</span>
    </div>
  </div>

</div>
<!-- ── END BANNER ── -->


<!-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     CASE STUDY BODY
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ -->
<div class="case-body">

  <div class="case-section">
    <div class="case-section-label">01 / Overview</div>
    <h3>What is Traway?</h3>
    <p>
      Traway was a student-led mobility and events startup based in Srinagar, India.
      As Co-founder and CEO, I built and scaled the venture alongside TheLoudKitchens,
      leading a cross-functional team of 10+ members and executing 175+ events over
      18 months.
    </p>
    <p>
      The analytics work focused on understanding customer behaviour at the cohort
      level — who stayed, who churned, and what pricing interventions could move
      the needle on retention and order value.
    </p>
  </div>

  <div class="case-section">
    <div class="case-section-label">02 / Problem</div>
    <h3>The retention problem</h3>
    <p>
      With 3,000+ customers acquired across two ventures, we had significant volume
      but no systematic view of which cohorts returned, at what frequency, and why
      certain pricing tiers drove higher lifetime value.
    </p>
    <p>
      Without data-driven segmentation, every marketing and pricing decision was
      intuition-based — and the gap between our best and worst cohorts was wide.
    </p>
  </div>

  <div class="case-section">
    <div class="case-section-label">03 / Approach</div>
    <h3>Cohort analysis + pricing regression</h3>
    <ul>
      <li>Built cohort retention tables segmented by acquisition channel and event type using Python and SQL.</li>
      <li>Ran regression analysis to quantify the relationship between pricing tiers, event size, and customer LTV.</li>
      <li>Applied RFM (Recency, Frequency, Monetary) segmentation across 3,000+ customer records.</li>
      <li>Identified high-value segments and built a pricing ladder that reduced churn in the top cohort by targeting repeat-purchase incentives.</li>
      <li>Used EDA to surface seasonal patterns and inform event scheduling decisions.</li>
    </ul>
  </div>

  <div class="case-section">
    <div class="case-section-label">04 / Results</div>
    <h3>Measurable business impact</h3>

    <div class="result-grid">
      <div class="result-card">
        <span class="result-num">60%+</span>
        <span class="result-label">Customer retention rate</span>
      </div>
      <div class="result-card">
        <span class="result-num">3×</span>
        <span class="result-label">Increase in average order value (TheLoudKitchens)</span>
      </div>
      <div class="result-card">
        <span class="result-num">175+</span>
        <span class="result-label">Events executed over 18 months</span>
      </div>
    </div>

    <p style="margin-top:24px">
      The segmentation work directly informed pricing decisions that tripled AOV
      for TheLoudKitchens and established a repeatable retention playbook for Traway.
    </p>
  </div>

  <div class="case-section">
    <div class="case-section-label">05 / Learnings</div>
    <h3>What I would do differently</h3>
    <p>
      At the time, data collection was opportunistic rather than designed. With more
      instrumentation from day one — event-level tracking, referral source tagging —
      the cohort analysis would have been far more precise. This shaped how I think
      about analytics infrastructure in every subsequent project.
    </p>
  </div>

</div><!-- /case-body -->


<div class="toast" id="toast">✓ Copied to clipboard</div>
```
